revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[bcl:Toriyama Sekien]][[bg:Торияма Секиен]][[ca:Toriyama Sekien]][[de:Toriyama Sekien]][[el:Τοριγιάμα Σέκιεν]][[en:Toriyama Sekien]][[es:Toriyama Sekien]][[fa:توری‌یاما سکیئن]][[fr:Toriyama Sekien]][[id:Toriyama Sekien]][[it:Toriyama Sekien]][[ja:鳥山石燕]][[ko:도리야마 세키엔]][[nl:Toriyama Sekien]][[no:Toriyama Sekien]][[pl:Sekien Toriyama]][[pt:Toriyama Sekien]][[ru:Торияма Сэкиэн]][[tr:Toriyama Seiken]][[uk:Торіяма Секіен]][[vi:Toriyama Sekien]][[zh:鳥山石燕]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1712|death_date={{death date|1788|9|22}}|death_place={{ill|Kamakura|qid=Q200267}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|qid=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|haiku poet|qid=Q11386386}}; {{ill|painter|qid=Q1028181}}; {{ill|ukiyo-e artist|qid=Q33082999}}|notable_works={{ill|Gazu Hyakki Tsurezure Bukuro|qid=Q2703856}}; {{ill|Gazu Hyakki Yagyō|qid=Q2696876}}; {{ill|Konjaku Gazu Zoku Hyakki|qid=Q2703416}}; {{ill|Konjaku Hyakki Shūi|qid=Q2703425}}|native_name=鳥山石燕|resting_place={{ill|Kōmyō-ji|qid=Q111974576}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q3284204]] Wikidata name: Toriyama Sekien [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese artist (1712–1788)}} |italic=no}}, specifically a [[:en:Miage-nyūdō|Miage-nyūdō]], as portrayed by Toriyama]] {{nihongo|'''Toriyama Sekien'''|鳥山 石燕|extra=1712 – September 22, 1788}}, real name '''Sano Toyofusa''', was a scholar, ''[[:en:Kyōka|Kyōka]]'' poet, and ''[[ukiyo-e]]'' artist of [[Japanese folklore]]. Born to a family of high-Ranking servants to the [[Tokugawa shogunate]], he was trained by [[:en:Kanō school|Kanō school]] artists Kanō Gyokuen and Kanō Chikanobu, although he was never officially recognized as a Kanō school painter.<ref>{{cite book | last = Davis | first = Julie | title = Partners in Print: Artistic Collaboration and the Ukiyo-e Market | publisher = University of Hawaii Press | year = 2015 | page = 25 | url=http://www.uhpress.hawaii.edu/p-9337-9780824839383.aspx | isbn = 978-0-8248-3938-3 }}</ref>
==Art career== After retiring from service to the shogunate, he became a teacher to numerous apprentices in poetry and painting. He was among the first to apply Kanō techniques to ''[[ukiyo-e]]'' printmaking, inventing key new techniques along the way, such as ''fuki-bokashi'', which allowed for replicating color gradations.<ref>{{cite book | last = Davis | first = Julie | title = Utamaro and the Spectacle of Beauty | publisher = University of Hawaii Press | year = 2007 | page = 33 | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=oZTrAAAAMAAJ&q=fuki-bokashi+sekien | isbn = 978-0-8248-3199-8 }}</ref> Most famously, he was the teacher of [[:en:Kitagawa Utamaro|Kitagawa Utamaro]] and [[:en:Utagawa Toyoharu|Utagawa Toyoharu]].<ref>{{cite book | last1 = Yoda | first1 = Hiroko | last2 = Alt | first2 = Matt |title = Japandemonium Illustrated: The Yokai Encyclopedias of Toriyama Sekien | publisher = Dover Publications | year = 2017 | page = vii | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Hj9-rgEACAAJ | isbn = 978-0-4868-0035-6 }}</ref>
Sekien is best known for his mass-produced illustrated books of [[:en:Yōkai|Yōkai]] that had appeared in ''[[:en:Hyakki Yagyō|Hyakki Yagyō]]'' monster parade scrolls. The first book proved popular enough to spawn three sequels, the last of which features yōkai mainly out of Sekien's imagination. Although sometimes described as a "demonologist," his work is better described as a literary parody of encyclopedias such as the Japanese ''[[:en:Wakan Sansai Zue|Wakan Sansai Zue]]'' or the Chinese ''[[:en:Classic of Mountains and Seas|Classic of Mountains and Seas]]'', which were popular in Japan at the time.<ref>{{cite book | last1 = Yoda | first1 = Hiroko | last2 = Alt | first2 = Matt | title = Japandemonium Illustrated: The Yokai Encyclopedias of Toriyama Sekien | publisher = Dover Publications | year = 2017 | pages = viii-ix| url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Hj9-rgEACAAJ | isbn = 978-0-4868-0035-6 }}</ref> His portrayals of these creatures from folklore essentially established their visual portrayals in the public's mind and deeply inspired other Japanese artists in his own and later eras, including ukiyo-e artists [[:en:Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi]], [[:en:Kawanabe Kyōsai|Kawanabe Kyōsai]], and [[:en:Manga|Manga]] artist [[:en:Mizuki Shigeru|Mizuki Shigeru]].<ref>{{cite book | last1 = Yoda | first1 = Hiroko | last2 = Alt | first2 = Matt | title = Japandemonium Illustrated: The Yokai Encyclopedias of Toriyama Sekien | publisher = Dover Publications | year = 2017 | page = ix | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Hj9-rgEACAAJ | isbn = 978-0-4868-0035-6 }}</ref>
==Notable works== *{{nihongo|''[[Toriyamabiko]]''|鳥山彦|extra=published in 1773}} *{{nihongo|''[[Sekien Gafu|Sekien's Picture Album]]''|石燕画譜|extra=published in 1774}} *{{nihongo|''[[:en:Gazu Hyakki Yagyō|The Illustrated Demon Horde's Night Parade]]''|画図百鬼夜行|extra=published in 1776}} *{{nihongo|''[[:en:Konjaku Gazu Zoku Hyakki|The Illustrated Demon Horde from Past and Present, Continued]]''|今昔画図続百鬼|extra=published in 1779}} *{{nihongo|''[[:en:Konjaku Hyakki Shūi|More of the Demon Horde from Past and Present]]''|今昔百鬼拾遺|extra=published in 1780}} *Kawataro 河太郎 *Kawako 河腰 *Yamawaro 山和露 *Ushi no koku mairi 牛の国参り * * *{{nihongo|''[[:en:Gazu Hyakki Tsurezure Bukuro|A Horde of Haunted Housewares]]''|画図百鬼徒然袋|extra=published in 1784}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://www.mfa.org/collections/object/toriyamabiko-528443 Toriyamabiko], a copy of his first book, in the collection of the Museum of Fine Arts, Boston *[http://libmma.contentdm.oclc.org/cdm/compoundobject/collection/p15324coll10/id/153737 Bridge of dreams: the Mary Griggs Burke collection of Japanese art], a catalog from The Metropolitan Museum of Art Libraries (fully available online as PDF), which contains material on Toriyama Sekien (see index)
{{commons Category|Toriyama Sekien}} {{Ukiyo-e artists}} {{Authority control}} ‎]]]] {{japan-artist-stub}} {{printmaker-stub}}

}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[bcl:Toriyama Sekien]][[bg:Торияма Секиен]][[ca:Toriyama Sekien]][[de:Toriyama Sekien]][[el:Τοριγιάμα Σέκιεν]][[en:Toriyama Sekien]][[es:Toriyama Sekien]][[fa:توری‌یاما سکیئن]][[fr:Toriyama Sekien]][[id:Toriyama Sekien]][[it:Toriyama Sekien]][[ja:鳥山石燕]][[ko:도리야마 세키엔]][[nl:Toriyama Sekien]][[no:Toriyama Sekien]][[pl:Sekien Toriyama]][[pt:Toriyama Sekien]][[ru:Торияма Сэкиэн]][[tr:Toriyama Seiken]][[uk:Торіяма Секіен]][[vi:Toriyama Sekien]][[zh:鳥山石燕]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1712|death_date={{death date|1788|9|22}}|death_place={{ill|Kamakura|WD=Q200267}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|haiku poet|WD=Q11386386}}; {{ill|painter|WD=Q1028181}}; {{ill|ukiyo-e artist|WD=Q33082999}}|notable_works={{ill|Gazu Hyakki Tsurezure Bukuro|WD=Q2703856}}; {{ill|Gazu Hyakki Yagyō|WD=Q2696876}}; {{ill|Konjaku Gazu Zoku Hyakki|WD=Q2703416}}; {{ill|Konjaku Hyakki Shūi|WD=Q2703425}}|native_name=鳥山石燕|resting_place={{ill|Kōmyō-ji|WD=Q111974576}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q3284204]] Wikidata name: Toriyama Sekien [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese artist (1712–1788)}} |italic=no}}, specifically a [[:en:Miage-nyūdō|Miage-nyūdō]], as portrayed by Toriyama]] {{nihongo|'''Toriyama Sekien'''|鳥山 石燕|extra=1712 – September 22, 1788}}, real name '''Sano Toyofusa''', was a scholar, ''[[:en:Kyōka|Kyōka]]'' poet, and ''[[ukiyo-e]]'' artist of [[Japanese folklore]]. Born to a family of high-Ranking servants to the [[Tokugawa shogunate]], he was trained by [[:en:Kanō school|Kanō school]] artists Kanō Gyokuen and Kanō Chikanobu, although he was never officially recognized as a Kanō school painter.<ref>{{cite book | last = Davis | first = Julie | title = Partners in Print: Artistic Collaboration and the Ukiyo-e Market | publisher = University of Hawaii Press | year = 2015 | page = 25 | url=http://www.uhpress.hawaii.edu/p-9337-9780824839383.aspx | isbn = 978-0-8248-3938-3 }}</ref>
==Art career== After retiring from service to the shogunate, he became a teacher to numerous apprentices in poetry and painting. He was among the first to apply Kanō techniques to ''[[ukiyo-e]]'' printmaking, inventing key new techniques along the way, such as ''fuki-bokashi'', which allowed for replicating color gradations.<ref>{{cite book | last = Davis | first = Julie | title = Utamaro and the Spectacle of Beauty | publisher = University of Hawaii Press | year = 2007 | page = 33 | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=oZTrAAAAMAAJ&q=fuki-bokashi+sekien | isbn = 978-0-8248-3199-8 }}</ref> Most famously, he was the teacher of [[:en:Kitagawa Utamaro|Kitagawa Utamaro]] and [[:en:Utagawa Toyoharu|Utagawa Toyoharu]].<ref>{{cite book | last1 = Yoda | first1 = Hiroko | last2 = Alt | first2 = Matt |title = Japandemonium Illustrated: The Yokai Encyclopedias of Toriyama Sekien | publisher = Dover Publications | year = 2017 | page = vii | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Hj9-rgEACAAJ | isbn = 978-0-4868-0035-6 }}</ref>
Sekien is best known for his mass-produced illustrated books of [[:en:Yōkai|Yōkai]] that had appeared in ''[[:en:Hyakki Yagyō|Hyakki Yagyō]]'' monster parade scrolls. The first book proved popular enough to spawn three sequels, the last of which features yōkai mainly out of Sekien's imagination. Although sometimes described as a "demonologist," his work is better described as a literary parody of encyclopedias such as the Japanese ''[[:en:Wakan Sansai Zue|Wakan Sansai Zue]]'' or the Chinese ''[[:en:Classic of Mountains and Seas|Classic of Mountains and Seas]]'', which were popular in Japan at the time.<ref>{{cite book | last1 = Yoda | first1 = Hiroko | last2 = Alt | first2 = Matt | title = Japandemonium Illustrated: The Yokai Encyclopedias of Toriyama Sekien | publisher = Dover Publications | year = 2017 | pages = viii-ix| url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Hj9-rgEACAAJ | isbn = 978-0-4868-0035-6 }}</ref> His portrayals of these creatures from folklore essentially established their visual portrayals in the public's mind and deeply inspired other Japanese artists in his own and later eras, including ukiyo-e artists [[:en:Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi]], [[:en:Kawanabe Kyōsai|Kawanabe Kyōsai]], and [[:en:Manga|Manga]] artist [[:en:Mizuki Shigeru|Mizuki Shigeru]].<ref>{{cite book | last1 = Yoda | first1 = Hiroko | last2 = Alt | first2 = Matt | title = Japandemonium Illustrated: The Yokai Encyclopedias of Toriyama Sekien | publisher = Dover Publications | year = 2017 | page = ix | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Hj9-rgEACAAJ | isbn = 978-0-4868-0035-6 }}</ref>
==Notable works== *{{nihongo|''[[Toriyamabiko]]''|鳥山彦|extra=published in 1773}} *{{nihongo|''[[Sekien Gafu|Sekien's Picture Album]]''|石燕画譜|extra=published in 1774}} *{{nihongo|''[[:en:Gazu Hyakki Yagyō|The Illustrated Demon Horde's Night Parade]]''|画図百鬼夜行|extra=published in 1776}} *{{nihongo|''[[:en:Konjaku Gazu Zoku Hyakki|The Illustrated Demon Horde from Past and Present, Continued]]''|今昔画図続百鬼|extra=published in 1779}} *{{nihongo|''[[:en:Konjaku Hyakki Shūi|More of the Demon Horde from Past and Present]]''|今昔百鬼拾遺|extra=published in 1780}} *Kawataro 河太郎 *Kawako 河腰 *Yamawaro 山和露 *Ushi no koku mairi 牛の国参り * * *{{nihongo|''[[:en:Gazu Hyakki Tsurezure Bukuro|A Horde of Haunted Housewares]]''|画図百鬼徒然袋|extra=published in 1784}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://www.mfa.org/collections/object/toriyamabiko-528443 Toriyamabiko], a copy of his first book, in the collection of the Museum of Fine Arts, Boston *[http://libmma.contentdm.oclc.org/cdm/compoundobject/collection/p15324coll10/id/153737 Bridge of dreams: the Mary Griggs Burke collection of Japanese art], a catalog from The Metropolitan Museum of Art Libraries (fully available online as PDF), which contains material on Toriyama Sekien (see index)
{{commons Category|Toriyama Sekien}} {{Ukiyo-e artists}} {{Authority control}} ‎]]]] {{japan-artist-stub}} {{printmaker-stub}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[bcl:Toriyama Sekien]][[bg:Торияма Секиен]][[ca:Toriyama Sekien]][[de:Toriyama Sekien]][[el:Τοριγιάμα Σέκιεν]][[en:Toriyama Sekien]][[es:Toriyama Sekien]][[fa:توری‌یاما سکیئن]][[fr:Toriyama Sekien]][[id:Toriyama Sekien]][[it:Toriyama Sekien]][[ja:鳥山石燕]][[ko:도리야마 세키엔]][[nl:Toriyama Sekien]][[no:Toriyama Sekien]][[pl:Sekien Toriyama]][[pt:Toriyama Sekien]][[ru:Торияма Сэкиэн]][[tr:Toriyama Seiken]][[uk:Торіяма Секіен]][[vi:Toriyama Sekien]][[zh:鳥山石燕]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1712|death_date={{death date|1788|9|22}}|death_place={{ill|Kamakura|WD=Q200267}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|haiku poet|WD=Q11386386}}; {{ill|painter|WD=Q1028181}}; {{ill|ukiyo-e artist|WD=Q33082999}}|notable_works={{ill|Gazu Hyakki Tsurezure Bukuro|WD=Q2703856}}; {{ill|Gazu Hyakki Yagyō|WD=Q2696876}}; {{ill|Konjaku Gazu Zoku Hyakki|WD=Q2703416}}; {{ill|Konjaku Hyakki Shūi|WD=Q2703425}}|native_name=鳥山石燕|resting_place={{ill|Kōmyō-ji|WD=Q111974576}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q3284204]] Wikidata name: Toriyama Sekien [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese artist (1712–1788)}} |italic=no}}, specifically a [[:en:Miage-nyūdō|Miage-nyūdō]], as portrayed by Toriyama]] {{nihongo|'''Toriyama Sekien'''|鳥山 石燕|extra=1712 – September 22, 1788}}, real name '''Sano Toyofusa''', was a scholar, ''[[:en:Kyōka|Kyōka]]'' poet, and ''[[ukiyo-e]]'' artist of [[Japanese folklore]]. Born to a family of high-Ranking servants to the [[Tokugawa shogunate]], he was trained by [[:en:Kanō school|Kanō school]] artists Kanō Gyokuen and Kanō Chikanobu, although he was never officially recognized as a Kanō school painter.<ref>{{cite book | last = Davis | first = Julie | title = Partners in Print: Artistic Collaboration and the Ukiyo-e Market | publisher = University of Hawaii Press | year = 2015 | page = 25 | url=http://www.uhpress.hawaii.edu/p-9337-9780824839383.aspx | isbn = 978-0-8248-3938-3 }}</ref>
==Art career== After retiring from service to the shogunate, he became a teacher to numerous apprentices in poetry and painting. He was among the first to apply Kanō techniques to ''[[ukiyo-e]]'' printmaking, inventing key new techniques along the way, such as ''fuki-bokashi'', which allowed for replicating color gradations.<ref>{{cite book | last = Davis | first = Julie | title = Utamaro and the Spectacle of Beauty | publisher = University of Hawaii Press | year = 2007 | page = 33 | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=oZTrAAAAMAAJ&q=fuki-bokashi+sekien | isbn = 978-0-8248-3199-8 }}</ref> Most famously, he was the teacher of [[:en:Kitagawa Utamaro|Kitagawa Utamaro]] and [[:en:Utagawa Toyoharu|Utagawa Toyoharu]].<ref>{{cite book | last1 = Yoda | first1 = Hiroko | last2 = Alt | first2 = Matt |title = Japandemonium Illustrated: The Yokai Encyclopedias of Toriyama Sekien | publisher = Dover Publications | year = 2017 | page = vii | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Hj9-rgEACAAJ | isbn = 978-0-4868-0035-6 }}</ref>
Sekien is best known for his mass-produced illustrated books of [[:en:Yōkai|Yōkai]] that had appeared in ''[[:en:Hyakki Yagyō|Hyakki Yagyō]]'' monster parade scrolls. The first book proved popular enough to spawn three sequels, the last of which features yōkai mainly out of Sekien's imagination. Although sometimes described as a "demonologist," his work is better described as a literary parody of encyclopedias such as the Japanese ''[[:en:Wakan Sansai Zue|Wakan Sansai Zue]]'' or the Chinese ''[[:en:Classic of Mountains and Seas|Classic of Mountains and Seas]]'', which were popular in Japan at the time.<ref>{{cite book | last1 = Yoda | first1 = Hiroko | last2 = Alt | first2 = Matt | title = Japandemonium Illustrated: The Yokai Encyclopedias of Toriyama Sekien | publisher = Dover Publications | year = 2017 | pages = viii-ix| url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Hj9-rgEACAAJ | isbn = 978-0-4868-0035-6 }}</ref> His portrayals of these creatures from folklore essentially established their visual portrayals in the public's mind and deeply inspired other Japanese artists in his own and later eras, including ukiyo-e artists [[:en:Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi]], [[:en:Kawanabe Kyōsai|Kawanabe Kyōsai]], and [[:en:Manga|Manga]] artist [[:en:Mizuki Shigeru|Mizuki Shigeru]].<ref>{{cite book | last1 = Yoda | first1 = Hiroko | last2 = Alt | first2 = Matt | title = Japandemonium Illustrated: The Yokai Encyclopedias of Toriyama Sekien | publisher = Dover Publications | year = 2017 | page = ix | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Hj9-rgEACAAJ | isbn = 978-0-4868-0035-6 }}</ref>
==Notable works== *{{nihongo|''[[Toriyamabiko]]''|鳥山彦|extra=published in 1773}} *{{nihongo|''[[Sekien Gafu|Sekien's Picture Album]]''|石燕画譜|extra=published in 1774}} *{{nihongo|''[[:en:Gazu Hyakki Yagyō|The Illustrated Demon Horde's Night Parade]]''|画図百鬼夜行|extra=published in 1776}} *{{nihongo|''[[:en:Konjaku Gazu Zoku Hyakki|The Illustrated Demon Horde from Past and Present, Continued]]''|今昔画図続百鬼|extra=published in 1779}} *{{nihongo|''[[:en:Konjaku Hyakki Shūi|More of the Demon Horde from Past and Present]]''|今昔百鬼拾遺|extra=published in 1780}} *Kawataro 河太郎 *Kawako 河腰 *Yamawaro 山和露 *Ushi no koku mairi 牛の国参り * * *{{nihongo|''[[:en:Gazu Hyakki Tsurezure Bukuro|A Horde of Haunted Housewares]]''|画図百鬼徒然袋|extra=published in 1784}}

==External links== *[http://www.mfa.org/collections/object/toriyamabiko-528443 Toriyamabiko], a copy of his first book, in the collection of the Museum of Fine Arts, Boston *[http://libmma.contentdm.oclc.org/cdm/compoundobject/collection/p15324coll10/id/153737 Bridge of dreams: the Mary Griggs Burke collection of Japanese art], a catalog from The Metropolitan Museum of Art Libraries (fully available online as PDF), which contains material on Toriyama Sekien (see index)

{{wikidata link|Q3284204}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[bcl:Toriyama Sekien]][[bg:Торияма Секиен]][[ca:Toriyama Sekien]][[de:Toriyama Sekien]][[el:Τοριγιάμα Σέκιεν]][[en:Toriyama Sekien]][[es:Toriyama Sekien]][[fa:توری‌یاما سکیئن]][[fr:Toriyama Sekien]][[id:Toriyama Sekien]][[it:Toriyama Sekien]][[ja:鳥山石燕]][[ko:도리야마 세키엔]][[nl:Toriyama Sekien]][[no:Toriyama Sekien]][[pl:Sekien Toriyama]][[pt:Toriyama Sekien]][[ru:Торияма Сэкиэн]][[tr:Toriyama Seiken]][[uk:Торіяма Секіен]][[vi:Toriyama Sekien]][[zh:鳥山石燕]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1712|death_date={{death date|1788|9|22}}|death_place={{ill|Kamakura|WD=Q200267}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|haiku poet|WD=Q11386386}}; {{ill|painter|WD=Q1028181}}; {{ill|ukiyo-e artist|WD=Q33082999}}|notable_works={{ill|Gazu Hyakki Tsurezure Bukuro|WD=Q2703856}}; {{ill|Gazu Hyakki Yagyō|WD=Q2696876}}; {{ill|Konjaku Gazu Zoku Hyakki|WD=Q2703416}}; {{ill|Konjaku Hyakki Shūi|WD=Q2703425}}|native_name=鳥山石燕|resting_place={{ill|Kōmyō-ji|WD=Q111974576}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q3284204]] Wikidata name: Toriyama Sekien [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese artist (1712–1788)}} |italic=no}}, specifically a [[:en:Miage-nyūdō|Miage-nyūdō]], as portrayed by Toriyama]] {{nihongo|'''Toriyama Sekien'''|鳥山 石燕|extra=1712 – September 22, 1788}}, real name '''Sano Toyofusa''', was a scholar, ''[[:en:Kyōka|Kyōka]]'' poet, and ''[[ukiyo-e]]'' artist of [[Japanese folklore]]. Born to a family of high-Ranking servants to the [[Tokugawa shogunate]], he was trained by [[:en:Kanō school|Kanō school]] artists Kanō Gyokuen and Kanō Chikanobu, although he was never officially recognized as a Kanō school painter.<ref>{{cite book | last = Davis | first = Julie | title = Partners in Print: Artistic Collaboration and the Ukiyo-e Market | publisher = University of Hawaii Press | year = 2015 | page = 25 | url=http://www.uhpress.hawaii.edu/p-9337-9780824839383.aspx | isbn = 978-0-8248-3938-3 }}</ref>
==Art career== After retiring from service to the shogunate, he became a teacher to numerous apprentices in poetry and painting. He was among the first to apply Kanō techniques to ''[[ukiyo-e]]'' printmaking, inventing key new techniques along the way, such as ''fuki-bokashi'', which allowed for replicating color gradations.<ref>{{cite book | last = Davis | first = Julie | title = Utamaro and the Spectacle of Beauty | publisher = University of Hawaii Press | year = 2007 | page = 33 | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=oZTrAAAAMAAJ&q=fuki-bokashi+sekien | isbn = 978-0-8248-3199-8 }}</ref> Most famously, he was the teacher of [[:en:Kitagawa Utamaro|Kitagawa Utamaro]] and [[:en:Utagawa Toyoharu|Utagawa Toyoharu]].<ref>{{cite book | last1 = Yoda | first1 = Hiroko | last2 = Alt | first2 = Matt |title = Japandemonium Illustrated: The Yokai Encyclopedias of Toriyama Sekien | publisher = Dover Publications | year = 2017 | page = vii | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Hj9-rgEACAAJ | isbn = 978-0-4868-0035-6 }}</ref>
Sekien is best known for his mass-produced illustrated books of [[:en:Yōkai|Yōkai]] that had appeared in ''[[:en:Hyakki Yagyō|Hyakki Yagyō]]'' monster parade scrolls. The first book proved popular enough to spawn three sequels, the last of which features yōkai mainly out of Sekien's imagination. Although sometimes described as a "demonologist," his work is better described as a literary parody of encyclopedias such as the Japanese ''[[:en:Wakan Sansai Zue|Wakan Sansai Zue]]'' or the Chinese ''[[:en:Classic of Mountains and Seas|Classic of Mountains and Seas]]'', which were popular in Japan at the time.<ref>{{cite book | last1 = Yoda | first1 = Hiroko | last2 = Alt | first2 = Matt | title = Japandemonium Illustrated: The Yokai Encyclopedias of Toriyama Sekien | publisher = Dover Publications | year = 2017 | pages = viii-ix| url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Hj9-rgEACAAJ | isbn = 978-0-4868-0035-6 }}</ref> His portrayals of these creatures from folklore essentially established their visual portrayals in the public's mind and deeply inspired other Japanese artists in his own and later eras, including ukiyo-e artists [[:en:Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi]], [[:en:Kawanabe Kyōsai|Kawanabe Kyōsai]], and [[:en:Manga|Manga]] artist [[:en:Mizuki Shigeru|Mizuki Shigeru]].<ref>{{cite book | last1 = Yoda | first1 = Hiroko | last2 = Alt | first2 = Matt | title = Japandemonium Illustrated: The Yokai Encyclopedias of Toriyama Sekien | publisher = Dover Publications | year = 2017 | page = ix | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Hj9-rgEACAAJ | isbn = 978-0-4868-0035-6 }}</ref>
==Notable works== *{{nihongo|''[[Toriyamabiko]]''|鳥山彦|extra=published in 1773}} *{{nihongo|''[[Sekien Gafu|Sekien's Picture Album]]''|石燕画譜|extra=published in 1774}} *{{nihongo|''[[:en:Gazu Hyakki Yagyō|The Illustrated Demon Horde's Night Parade]]''|画図百鬼夜行|extra=published in 1776}} *{{nihongo|''[[:en:Konjaku Gazu Zoku Hyakki|The Illustrated Demon Horde from Past and Present, Continued]]''|今昔画図続百鬼|extra=published in 1779}} *{{nihongo|''[[:en:Konjaku Hyakki Shūi|More of the Demon Horde from Past and Present]]''|今昔百鬼拾遺|extra=published in 1780}} *Kawataro 河太郎 *Kawako 河腰 *Yamawaro 山和露 *Ushi no koku mairi 牛の国参り * * *{{nihongo|''[[:en:Gazu Hyakki Tsurezure Bukuro|A Horde of Haunted Housewares]]''|画図百鬼徒然袋|extra=published in 1784}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://www.mfa.org/collections/object/toriyamabiko-528443 Toriyamabiko], a copy of his first book, in the collection of the Museum of Fine Arts, Boston *[http://libmma.contentdm.oclc.org/cdm/compoundobject/collection/p15324coll10/id/153737 Bridge of dreams: the Mary Griggs Burke collection of Japanese art], a catalog from The Metropolitan Museum of Art Libraries (fully available online as PDF), which contains material on Toriyama Sekien (see index)

Sekien is best known for his mass-produced illustrated books of [[:en:Yōkai|Yōkai]] that had appeared in ''[[:en:Hyakki Yagyō|Hyakki Yagyō]]'' monster parade scrolls. The first book proved popular enough to spawn three sequels, the last of which features yōkai mainly out of Sekien's imagination. Although sometimes described as a "demonologist," his work is better described as a literary parody of encyclopedias such as the Japanese ''[[:en:Wakan Sansai Zue|Wakan Sansai Zue]]'' or the Chinese ''[[:en:Classic of Mountains and Seas|Classic of Mountains and Seas]]'', which were popular in Japan at the time.<ref>{{cite book | last1 = Yoda | first1 = Hiroko | last2 = Alt | first2 = Matt | title = Japandemonium Illustrated: The Yokai Encyclopedias of Toriyama Sekien | publisher = Dover Publications | year = 2017 | pages = viii-ix| url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Hj9-rgEACAAJ | isbn = 978-0-4868-0035-6 }}</ref> His portrayals of these creatures from folklore essentially established their visual portrayals in the public's mind and deeply inspired other Japanese artists in his own and later eras, including ukiyo-e artists [[:en:Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi]], [[:en:Kawanabe Kyōsai|Kawanabe Kyōsai]], and [[:en:Manga|Manga]] artist [[:en:Mizuki Shigeru|Mizuki Shigeru]].<ref>{{cite book | last1 = Yoda | first1 = Hiroko | last2 = Alt | first2 = Matt | title = Japandemonium Illustrated: The Yokai Encyclopedias of Toriyama Sekien | publisher = Dover Publications | year = 2017 | page = ix | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Hj9-rgEACAAJ | isbn = 978-0-4868-0035-6 }}</ref>
==Notable works== *{{nihongo|''[[Toriyamabiko]]''|鳥山彦|extra=published in 1773}} *{{nihongo|''[[Sekien Gafu|Sekien's Picture Album]]''|石燕画譜|extra=published in 1774}} *{{nihongo|''[[:en:Gazu Hyakki Yagyō|The Illustrated Demon Horde's Night Parade]]''|画図百鬼夜行|extra=published in 1776}} *{{nihongo|''[[:en:Konjaku Gazu Zoku Hyakki|The Illustrated Demon Horde from Past and Present, Continued]]''|今昔画図続百鬼|extra=published in 1779}} *{{nihongo|''[[:en:Konjaku Hyakki Shūi|More of the Demon Horde from Past and Present]]''|今昔百鬼拾遺|extra=published in 1780}} *Kawataro 河太郎 *Kawako 河腰 *Yamawaro 山和露 *Ushi no koku mairi 牛の国参り * * *{{nihongo|''[[:en:Gazu Hyakki Tsurezure Bukuro|A Horde of Haunted Housewares]]''|画図百鬼徒然袋|extra=published in 1784}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://www.mfa.org/collections/object/toriyamabiko-528443 Toriyamabiko], a copy of his first book, in the collection of the Museum of Fine Arts, Boston *[http://libmma.contentdm.oclc.org/cdm/compoundobject/collection/p15324coll10/id/153737 Bridge of dreams: the Mary Griggs Burke collection of Japanese art], a catalog from The Metropolitan Museum of Art Libraries (fully available online as PDF), which contains material on Toriyama Sekien (see index)

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[bcl:Toriyama Sekien]][[bg:Торияма Секиен]][[ca:Toriyama Sekien]][[de:Toriyama Sekien]][[el:Τοριγιάμα Σέκιεν]][[en:Toriyama Sekien]][[es:Toriyama Sekien]][[fa:توری‌یاما سکیئن]][[fr:Toriyama Sekien]][[id:Toriyama Sekien]][[it:Toriyama Sekien]][[ja:鳥山石燕]][[ko:도리야마 세키엔]][[nl:Toriyama Sekien]][[no:Toriyama Sekien]][[pl:Sekien Toriyama]][[pt:Toriyama Sekien]][[ru:Торияма Сэкиэн]][[tr:Toriyama Seiken]][[uk:Торіяма Секіен]][[vi:Toriyama Sekien]][[zh:鳥山石燕]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1712|death_date={{death date|1788|9|22}}|death_place={{ill|Kamakura|WD=Q200267}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|haiku poet|WD=Q11386386}}; {{ill|painter|WD=Q1028181}}; {{ill|ukiyo-e artist|WD=Q33082999}}|notable_works={{ill|Gazu Hyakki Tsurezure Bukuro|WD=Q2703856}}; {{ill|Gazu Hyakki Yagyō|WD=Q2696876}}; {{ill|Konjaku Gazu Zoku Hyakki|WD=Q2703416}}; {{ill|Konjaku Hyakki Shūi|WD=Q2703425}}|native_name=鳥山石燕|resting_place={{ill|Kōmyō-ji|WD=Q111974576}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q3284204]] Wikidata name: Toriyama Sekien [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese artist (1712–1788)}} |italic=no}}, specifically a [[:en:Miage-nyūdō|Miage-nyūdō]], as portrayed by Toriyama]] {{nihongo|'''Toriyama Sekien'''|鳥山 石燕|extra=1712 – September 22, 1788}}, real name '''Sano Toyofusa''', was a scholar, ''[[:en:Kyōka|Kyōka]]'' poet, and ''[[ukiyo-e]]'' artist of [[Japanese folklore]]. Born to a family of high-Ranking servants to the [[Tokugawa shogunate]], he was trained by [[:en:Kanō school|Kanō school]] artists Kanō Gyokuen and Kanō Chikanobu, although he was never officially recognized as a Kanō school painter.<ref>{{cite book | last = Davis | first = Julie | title = Partners in Print: Artistic Collaboration and the Ukiyo-e Market | publisher = University of Hawaii Press | year = 2015 | page = 25 | url=http://www.uhpress.hawaii.edu/p-9337-9780824839383.aspx | isbn = 978-0-8248-3938-3 }}</ref>
==Art career== After retiring from service to the shogunate, he became a teacher to numerous apprentices in poetry and painting. He was among the first to apply Kanō techniques to ''[[ukiyo-e]]'' printmaking, inventing key new techniques along the way, such as ''fuki-bokashi'', which allowed for replicating color gradations.<ref>{{cite book | last = Davis | first = Julie | title = Utamaro and the Spectacle of Beauty | publisher = University of Hawaii Press | year = 2007 | page = 33 | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=oZTrAAAAMAAJ&q=fuki-bokashi+sekien | isbn = 978-0-8248-3199-8 }}</ref> Most famously, he was the teacher of [[:en:Kitagawa Utamaro|Kitagawa Utamaro]] and [[:en:Utagawa Toyoharu|Utagawa Toyoharu]].<ref>{{cite book | last1 = Yoda | first1 = Hiroko | last2 = Alt | first2 = Matt |title = Japandemonium Illustrated: The Yokai Encyclopedias of Toriyama Sekien | publisher = Dover Publications | year = 2017 | page = vii | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Hj9-rgEACAAJ | isbn = 978-0-4868-0035-6 }}</ref>
Sekien is best known for his mass-produced illustrated books of [[:en:Yōkai|Yōkai]] that had appeared in ''[[:en:Hyakki Yagyō|Hyakki Yagyō]]'' monster parade scrolls. The first book proved popular enough to spawn three sequels, the last of which features yōkai mainly out of Sekien's imagination. Although sometimes described as a "demonologist," his work is better described as a literary parody of encyclopedias such as the Japanese ''[[:en:Wakan Sansai Zue|Wakan Sansai Zue]]'' or the Chinese ''[[:en:Classic of Mountains and Seas|Classic of Mountains and Seas]]'', which were popular in Japan at the time.<ref>{{cite book | last1 = Yoda | first1 = Hiroko | last2 = Alt | first2 = Matt | title = Japandemonium Illustrated: The Yokai Encyclopedias of Toriyama Sekien | publisher = Dover Publications | year = 2017 | pages = viii-ix| url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Hj9-rgEACAAJ | isbn = 978-0-4868-0035-6 }}</ref> His portrayals of these creatures from folklore essentially established their visual portrayals in the public's mind and deeply inspired other Japanese artists in his own and later eras, including ukiyo-e artists [[:en:Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi]], [[:en:Kawanabe Kyōsai|Kawanabe Kyōsai]], and [[:en:Manga|Manga]] artist [[:en:Mizuki Shigeru|Mizuki Shigeru]].<ref>{{cite book | last1 = Yoda | first1 = Hiroko | last2 = Alt | first2 = Matt | title = Japandemonium Illustrated: The Yokai Encyclopedias of Toriyama Sekien | publisher = Dover Publications | year = 2017 | page = ix | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Hj9-rgEACAAJ | isbn = 978-0-4868-0035-6 }}</ref>
==Notable works== *{{nihongo|''[[Toriyamabiko]]''|鳥山彦|extra=published in 1773}} *{{nihongo|''[[Sekien Gafu|Sekien's Picture Album]]''|石燕画譜|extra=published in 1774}} *{{nihongo|''[[:en:Gazu Hyakki Yagyō|The Illustrated Demon Horde's Night Parade]]''|画図百鬼夜行|extra=published in 1776}} *{{nihongo|''[[:en:Konjaku Gazu Zoku Hyakki|The Illustrated Demon Horde from Past and Present, Continued]]''|今昔画図続百鬼|extra=published in 1779}} *{{nihongo|''[[:en:Konjaku Hyakki Shūi|More of the Demon Horde from Past and Present]]''|今昔百鬼拾遺|extra=published in 1780}} *Kawataro 河太郎 *Kawako 河腰 *Yamawaro 山和露 *Ushi no koku mairi 牛の国参り * * *{{nihongo|''[[:en:Gazu Hyakki Tsurezure Bukuro|A Horde of Haunted Housewares]]''|画図百鬼徒然袋|extra=published in 1784}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://www.mfa.org/collections/object/toriyamabiko-528443 Toriyamabiko], a copy of his first book, in the collection of the Museum of Fine Arts, Boston *[http://libmma.contentdm.oclc.org/cdm/compoundobject/collection/p15324coll10/id/153737 Bridge of dreams: the Mary Griggs Burke collection of Japanese art], a catalog from The Metropolitan Museum of Art Libraries (fully available online as PDF), which contains material on Toriyama Sekien (see index)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[bcl:Toriyama Sekien]][[bg:Торияма Секиен]][[ca:Toriyama Sekien]][[de:Toriyama Sekien]][[el:Τοριγιάμα Σέκιεν]][[en:Toriyama Sekien]][[es:Toriyama Sekien]][[fa:توری‌یاما سکیئن]][[fr:Toriyama Sekien]][[id:Toriyama Sekien]][[it:Toriyama Sekien]][[ja:鳥山石燕]][[ko:도리야마 세키엔]][[nl:Toriyama Sekien]][[no:Toriyama Sekien]][[pl:Sekien Toriyama]][[pt:Toriyama Sekien]][[ru:Торияма Сэкиэн]][[tr:Toriyama Seiken]][[uk:Торіяма Секіен]][[vi:Toriyama Sekien]][[zh:鳥山石燕]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1712|death_date={{death date|1788|9|22}}|death_place={{ill|Kamakura|WD=Q200267}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|haiku poet|WD=Q11386386}}; {{ill|painter|WD=Q1028181}}; {{ill|ukiyo-e artist|WD=Q33082999}}|notable_works={{ill|Gazu Hyakki Tsurezure Bukuro|WD=Q2703856}}; {{ill|Gazu Hyakki Yagyō|WD=Q2696876}}; {{ill|Konjaku Gazu Zoku Hyakki|WD=Q2703416}}; {{ill|Konjaku Hyakki Shūi|WD=Q2703425}}|native_name=鳥山石燕|resting_place={{ill|Kōmyō-ji|WD=Q111974576}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q3284204]] Wikidata name: Toriyama Sekien [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese artist (1712–1788)}} |italic=no}}, specifically a [[:en:Miage-nyūdō|Miage-nyūdō]], as portrayed by Toriyama]] {{nihongo|'''Toriyama Sekien'''|鳥山 石燕|extra=1712 – September 22, 1788}}, real name '''Sano Toyofusa''', was a scholar, ''[[:en:Kyōka|Kyōka]]'' poet, and ''[[ukiyo-e]]'' artist of [[Japanese folklore]]. Born to a family of high-Ranking servants to the [[Tokugawa shogunate]], he was trained by [[:en:Kanō school|Kanō school]] artists Kanō Gyokuen and Kanō Chikanobu, although he was never officially recognized as a Kanō school painter.<ref>{{cite book | last = Davis | first = Julie | title = Partners in Print: Artistic Collaboration and the Ukiyo-e Market | publisher = University of Hawaii Press | year = 2015 | page = 25 | url=http://www.uhpress.hawaii.edu/p-9337-9780824839383.aspx | isbn = 978-0-8248-3938-3 }}</ref>
==Art career== After retiring from service to the shogunate, he became a teacher to numerous apprentices in poetry and painting. He was among the first to apply Kanō techniques to ''[[ukiyo-e]]'' printmaking, inventing key new techniques along the way, such as ''fuki-bokashi'', which allowed for replicating color gradations.<ref>{{cite book | last = Davis | first = Julie | title = Utamaro and the Spectacle of Beauty | publisher = University of Hawaii Press | year = 2007 | page = 33 | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=oZTrAAAAMAAJ&q=fuki-bokashi+sekien | isbn = 978-0-8248-3199-8 }}</ref> Most famously, he was the teacher of [[:en:Kitagawa Utamaro|Kitagawa Utamaro]] and [[:en:Utagawa Toyoharu|Utagawa Toyoharu]].<ref>{{cite book | last1 = Yoda | first1 = Hiroko | last2 = Alt | first2 = Matt |title = Japandemonium Illustrated: The Yokai Encyclopedias of Toriyama Sekien | publisher = Dover Publications | year = 2017 | page = vii | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Hj9-rgEACAAJ | isbn = 978-0-4868-0035-6 }}</ref>
Sekien is best known for his mass-produced illustrated books of [[:en:Yōkai|Yōkai]] that had appeared in ''[[:en:Hyakki Yagyō|Hyakki Yagyō]]'' monster parade scrolls. The first book proved popular enough to spawn three sequels, the last of which features yōkai mainly out of Sekien's imagination. Although sometimes described as a "demonologist," his work is better described as a literary parody of encyclopedias such as the Japanese ''[[:en:Wakan Sansai Zue|Wakan Sansai Zue]]'' or the Chinese ''[[:en:Classic of Mountains and Seas|Classic of Mountains and Seas]]'', which were popular in Japan at the time.<ref>{{cite book | last1 = Yoda | first1 = Hiroko | last2 = Alt | first2 = Matt | title = Japandemonium Illustrated: The Yokai Encyclopedias of Toriyama Sekien | publisher = Dover Publications | year = 2017 | pages = viii-ix| url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Hj9-rgEACAAJ | isbn = 978-0-4868-0035-6 }}</ref> His portrayals of these creatures from folklore essentially established their visual portrayals in the public's mind and deeply inspired other Japanese artists in his own and later eras, including ukiyo-e artists [[:en:Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi]], [[:en:Kawanabe Kyōsai|Kawanabe Kyōsai]], and [[:en:Manga|Manga]] artist [[:en:Mizuki Shigeru|Mizuki Shigeru]].<ref>{{cite book | last1 = Yoda | first1 = Hiroko | last2 = Alt | first2 = Matt | title = Japandemonium Illustrated: The Yokai Encyclopedias of Toriyama Sekien | publisher = Dover Publications | year = 2017 | page = ix | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Hj9-rgEACAAJ | isbn = 978-0-4868-0035-6 }}</ref>
==Notable works== *{{nihongo|''[[Toriyamabiko]]''|鳥山彦|extra=published in 1773}} *{{nihongo|''[[Sekien Gafu|Sekien's Picture Album]]''|石燕画譜|extra=published in 1774}} *{{nihongo|''[[:en:Gazu Hyakki Yagyō|The Illustrated Demon Horde's Night Parade]]''|画図百鬼夜行|extra=published in 1776}} *{{nihongo|''[[:en:Konjaku Gazu Zoku Hyakki|The Illustrated Demon Horde from Past and Present, Continued]]''|今昔画図続百鬼|extra=published in 1779}} *{{nihongo|''[[:en:Konjaku Hyakki Shūi|More of the Demon Horde from Past and Present]]''|今昔百鬼拾遺|extra=published in 1780}} *Kawataro 河太郎 *Kawako 河腰 *Yamawaro 山和露 *Ushi no koku mairi 牛の国参り * * *{{nihongo|''[[:en:Gazu Hyakki Tsurezure Bukuro|A Horde of Haunted Housewares]]''|画図百鬼徒然袋|extra=published in 1784}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://www.mfa.org/collections/object/toriyamabiko-528443 Toriyamabiko], a copy of his first book, in the collection of the Museum of Fine Arts, Boston *[http://libmma.contentdm.oclc.org/cdm/compoundobject/collection/p15324coll10/id/153737 Bridge of dreams: the Mary Griggs Burke collection of Japanese art], a catalog from The Metropolitan Museum of Art Libraries (fully available online as PDF), which contains material on Toriyama Sekien (see index)
{{commons Category|Toriyama Sekien}} {{Ukiyo-e artists}} {{Authority control}} ‎]]]] {{japan-artist-stub}} {{printmaker-stub}}

}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[bcl:Toriyama Sekien]][[bg:Торияма Секиен]][[ca:Toriyama Sekien]][[de:Toriyama Sekien]][[el:Τοριγιάμα Σέκιεν]][[en:Toriyama Sekien]][[es:Toriyama Sekien]][[fa:توری‌یاما سکیئن]][[fr:Toriyama Sekien]][[id:Toriyama Sekien]][[it:Toriyama Sekien]][[ja:鳥山石燕]][[ko:도리야마 세키엔]][[nl:Toriyama Sekien]][[no:Toriyama Sekien]][[pl:Sekien Toriyama]][[pt:Toriyama Sekien]][[ru:Торияма Сэкиэн]][[tr:Toriyama Seiken]][[uk:Торіяма Секіен]][[vi:Toriyama Sekien]][[zh:鳥山石燕]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[bcl:Toriyama Sekien]][[bg:Торияма Секиен]][[ca:Toriyama Sekien]][[de:Toriyama Sekien]][[el:Τοριγιάμα Σέκιεν]][[en:Toriyama Sekien]][[es:Toriyama Sekien]][[fa:توری‌یاما سکیئن]][[fr:Toriyama Sekien]][[id:Toriyama Sekien]][[it:Toriyama Sekien]][[ja:鳥山石燕]][[ko:도리야마 세키엔]][[nl:Toriyama Sekien]][[no:Toriyama Sekien]][[pl:Sekien Toriyama]][[pt:Toriyama Sekien]][[ru:Торияма Сэкиэн]][[tr:Toriyama Seiken]][[uk:Торіяма Секіен]][[vi:Toriyama Sekien]][[zh:鳥山石燕]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[bcl:Toriyama Sekien]][[bg:Торияма Секиен]][[ca:Toriyama Sekien]][[de:Toriyama Sekien]][[el:Τοριγιάμα Σέκιεν]][[en:Toriyama Sekien]][[es:Toriyama Sekien]][[fa:توری‌یاما سکیئن]][[fr:Toriyama Sekien]][[id:Toriyama Sekien]][[it:Toriyama Sekien]][[ja:鳥山石燕]][[ko:도리야마 세키엔]][[nl:Toriyama Sekien]][[no:Toriyama Sekien]][[pl:Sekien Toriyama]][[pt:Toriyama Sekien]][[ru:Торияма Сэкиэн]][[tr:Toriyama Seiken]][[uk:Торіяма Секіен]][[vi:Toriyama Sekien]][[zh:鳥山石燕]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1712|death_date={{death date|1788|9|22}}|death_place={{ill|Kamakura|WD=Q200267}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|haiku poet|WD=Q11386386}}; {{ill|painter|WD=Q1028181}}; {{ill|ukiyo-e artist|WD=Q33082999}}|notable_works={{ill|Gazu Hyakki Tsurezure Bukuro|WD=Q2703856}}; {{ill|Gazu Hyakki Yagyō|WD=Q2696876}}; {{ill|Konjaku Gazu Zoku Hyakki|WD=Q2703416}}; {{ill|Konjaku Hyakki Shūi|WD=Q2703425}}|native_name=鳥山石燕|resting_place={{ill|Kōmyō-ji|WD=Q111974576}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q3284204]] Wikidata name: Toriyama Sekien [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese artist (1712–1788)}} |italic=no}}, specifically a [[:en:Miage-nyūdō|Miage-nyūdō]], as portrayed by Toriyama]] {{nihongo|'''Toriyama Sekien'''|鳥山 石燕|extra=1712 – September 22, 1788}}, real name '''Sano Toyofusa''', was a scholar, ''[[:en:Kyōka|Kyōka]]'' poet, and ''[[ukiyo-e]]'' artist of [[Japanese folklore]]. Born to a family of high-Ranking servants to the [[Tokugawa shogunate]], he was trained by [[:en:Kanō school|Kanō school]] artists Kanō Gyokuen and Kanō Chikanobu, although he was never officially recognized as a Kanō school painter.<ref>{{cite book | last = Davis | first = Julie | title = Partners in Print: Artistic Collaboration and the Ukiyo-e Market | publisher = University of Hawaii Press | year = 2015 | page = 25 | url=http://www.uhpress.hawaii.edu/p-9337-9780824839383.aspx | isbn = 978-0-8248-3938-3 }}</ref>
==Art career== After retiring from service to the shogunate, he became a teacher to numerous apprentices in poetry and painting. He was among the first to apply Kanō techniques to ''[[ukiyo-e]]'' printmaking, inventing key new techniques along the way, such as ''fuki-bokashi'', which allowed for replicating color gradations.<ref>{{cite book | last = Davis | first = Julie | title = Utamaro and the Spectacle of Beauty | publisher = University of Hawaii Press | year = 2007 | page = 33 | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=oZTrAAAAMAAJ&q=fuki-bokashi+sekien | isbn = 978-0-8248-3199-8 }}</ref> Most famously, he was the teacher of [[:en:Kitagawa Utamaro|Kitagawa Utamaro]] and [[:en:Utagawa Toyoharu|Utagawa Toyoharu]].<ref>{{cite book | last1 = Yoda | first1 = Hiroko | last2 = Alt | first2 = Matt |title = Japandemonium Illustrated: The Yokai Encyclopedias of Toriyama Sekien | publisher = Dover Publications | year = 2017 | page = vii | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Hj9-rgEACAAJ | isbn = 978-0-4868-0035-6 }}</ref>
Sekien is best known for his mass-produced illustrated books of [[:en:Yōkai|Yōkai]] that had appeared in ''[[:en:Hyakki Yagyō|Hyakki Yagyō]]'' monster parade scrolls. The first book proved popular enough to spawn three sequels, the last of which features yōkai mainly out of Sekien's imagination. Although sometimes described as a "demonologist," his work is better described as a literary parody of encyclopedias such as the Japanese ''[[:en:Wakan Sansai Zue|Wakan Sansai Zue]]'' or the Chinese ''[[:en:Classic of Mountains and Seas|Classic of Mountains and Seas]]'', which were popular in Japan at the time.<ref>{{cite book | last1 = Yoda | first1 = Hiroko | last2 = Alt | first2 = Matt | title = Japandemonium Illustrated: The Yokai Encyclopedias of Toriyama Sekien | publisher = Dover Publications | year = 2017 | pages = viii-ix| url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Hj9-rgEACAAJ | isbn = 978-0-4868-0035-6 }}</ref> His portrayals of these creatures from folklore essentially established their visual portrayals in the public's mind and deeply inspired other Japanese artists in his own and later eras, including ukiyo-e artists [[:en:Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi]], [[:en:Kawanabe Kyōsai|Kawanabe Kyōsai]], and [[:en:Manga|Manga]] artist [[:en:Mizuki Shigeru|Mizuki Shigeru]].<ref>{{cite book | last1 = Yoda | first1 = Hiroko | last2 = Alt | first2 = Matt | title = Japandemonium Illustrated: The Yokai Encyclopedias of Toriyama Sekien | publisher = Dover Publications | year = 2017 | page = ix | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Hj9-rgEACAAJ | isbn = 978-0-4868-0035-6 }}</ref>
==Notable works== *{{nihongo|''[[Toriyamabiko]]''|鳥山彦|extra=published in 1773}} *{{nihongo|''[[Sekien Gafu|Sekien's Picture Album]]''|石燕画譜|extra=published in 1774}} *{{nihongo|''[[:en:Gazu Hyakki Yagyō|The Illustrated Demon Horde's Night Parade]]''|画図百鬼夜行|extra=published in 1776}} *{{nihongo|''[[:en:Konjaku Gazu Zoku Hyakki|The Illustrated Demon Horde from Past and Present, Continued]]''|今昔画図続百鬼|extra=published in 1779}} *{{nihongo|''[[:en:Konjaku Hyakki Shūi|More of the Demon Horde from Past and Present]]''|今昔百鬼拾遺|extra=published in 1780}} *Kawataro 河太郎 *Kawako 河腰 *Yamawaro 山和露 *Ushi no koku mairi 牛の国参り * * *{{nihongo|''[[:en:Gazu Hyakki Tsurezure Bukuro|A Horde of Haunted Housewares]]''|画図百鬼徒然袋|extra=published in 1784}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://www.mfa.org/collections/object/toriyamabiko-528443 Toriyamabiko], a copy of his first book, in the collection of the Museum of Fine Arts, Boston *[http://libmma.contentdm.oclc.org/cdm/compoundobject/collection/p15324coll10/id/153737 Bridge of dreams: the Mary Griggs Burke collection of Japanese art], a catalog from The Metropolitan Museum of Art Libraries (fully available online as PDF), which contains material on Toriyama Sekien (see index)

Sekien is best known for his mass-produced illustrated books of [[:en:Yōkai|Yōkai]] that had appeared in ''[[:en:Hyakki Yagyō|Hyakki Yagyō]]'' monster parade scrolls. The first book proved popular enough to spawn three sequels, the last of which features yōkai mainly out of Sekien's imagination. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[bcl:Toriyama Sekien]][[bg:Торияма Секиен]][[ca:Toriyama Sekien]][[de:Toriyama Sekien]][[el:Τοριγιάμα Σέκιεν]][[en:Toriyama Sekien]][[es:Toriyama Sekien]][[fa:توری‌یاما سکیئن]][[fr:Toriyama Sekien]][[id:Toriyama Sekien]][[it:Toriyama Sekien]][[ja:鳥山石燕]][[ko:도리야마 세키엔]][[nl:Toriyama Sekien]][[no:Toriyama Sekien]][[pl:Sekien Toriyama]][[pt:Toriyama Sekien]][[ru:Торияма Сэкиэн]][[tr:Toriyama Seiken]][[uk:Торіяма Секіен]][[vi:Toriyama Sekien]][[zh:鳥山石燕]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[bcl:Toriyama Sekien]][[bg:Торияма Секиен]][[ca:Toriyama Sekien]][[de:Toriyama Sekien]][[el:Τοριγιάμα Σέκιεν]][[en:Toriyama Sekien]][[es:Toriyama Sekien]][[fa:توری‌یاما سکیئن]][[fr:Toriyama Sekien]][[id:Toriyama Sekien]][[it:Toriyama Sekien]][[ja:鳥山石燕]][[ko:도리야마 세키엔]][[nl:Toriyama Sekien]][[no:Toriyama Sekien]][[pl:Sekien Toriyama]][[pt:Toriyama Sekien]][[ru:Торияма Сэкиэн]][[tr:Toriyama Seiken]][[uk:Торіяма Секіен]][[vi:Toriyama Sekien]][[zh:鳥山石燕]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[bcl:Toriyama Sekien]][[bg:Торияма Секиен]][[ca:Toriyama Sekien]][[de:Toriyama Sekien]][[el:Τοριγιάμα Σέκιεν]][[en:Toriyama Sekien]][[es:Toriyama Sekien]][[fa:توری‌یاما سکیئن]][[fr:Toriyama Sekien]][[id:Toriyama Sekien]][[it:Toriyama Sekien]][[ja:鳥山石燕]][[ko:도리야마 세키엔]][[nl:Toriyama Sekien]][[no:Toriyama Sekien]][[pl:Sekien Toriyama]][[pt:Toriyama Sekien]][[ru:Торияма Сэкиэн]][[tr:Toriyama Seiken]][[uk:Торіяма Секіен]][[vi:Toriyama Sekien]][[zh:鳥山石燕]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1712|death_date={{death date|1788|9|22}}|death_place={{ill|Kamakura|WD=Q200267}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|haiku poet|WD=Q11386386}}; {{ill|painter|WD=Q1028181}}; {{ill|ukiyo-e artist|WD=Q33082999}}|notable_works={{ill|Gazu Hyakki Tsurezure Bukuro|WD=Q2703856}}; {{ill|Gazu Hyakki Yagyō|WD=Q2696876}}; {{ill|Konjaku Gazu Zoku Hyakki|WD=Q2703416}}; {{ill|Konjaku Hyakki Shūi|WD=Q2703425}}|native_name=鳥山石燕|resting_place={{ill|Kōmyō-ji|WD=Q111974576}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q3284204]] Wikidata name: Toriyama Sekien [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese artist (1712–1788)}} |italic=no}}, specifically a [[:en:Miage-nyūdō|Miage-nyūdō]], as portrayed by Toriyama]] {{nihongo|'''Toriyama Sekien'''|鳥山 石燕|extra=1712 – September 22, 1788}}, real name '''Sano Toyofusa''', was a scholar, ''[[:en:Kyōka|Kyōka]]'' poet, and ''[[ukiyo-e]]'' artist of [[Japanese folklore]]. Born to a family of high-Ranking servants to the [[Tokugawa shogunate]], he was trained by [[:en:Kanō school|Kanō school]] artists Kanō Gyokuen and Kanō Chikanobu, although he was never officially recognized as a Kanō school painter.<ref>{{cite book | last = Davis | first = Julie | title = Partners in Print: Artistic Collaboration and the Ukiyo-e Market | publisher = University of Hawaii Press | year = 2015 | page = 25 | url=http://www.uhpress.hawaii.edu/p-9337-9780824839383.aspx | isbn = 978-0-8248-3938-3 }}</ref>
==Art career== After retiring from service to the shogunate, he became a teacher to numerous apprentices in poetry and painting. He was among the first to apply Kanō techniques to ''[[ukiyo-e]]'' printmaking, inventing key new techniques along the way, such as ''fuki-bokashi'', which allowed for replicating color gradations.<ref>{{cite book | last = Davis | first = Julie | title = Utamaro and the Spectacle of Beauty | publisher = University of Hawaii Press | year = 2007 | page = 33 | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=oZTrAAAAMAAJ&q=fuki-bokashi+sekien | isbn = 978-0-8248-3199-8 }}</ref> Most famously, he was the teacher of [[:en:Kitagawa Utamaro|Kitagawa Utamaro]] and [[:en:Utagawa Toyoharu|Utagawa Toyoharu]].<ref>{{cite book | last1 = Yoda | first1 = Hiroko | last2 = Alt | first2 = Matt |title = Japandemonium Illustrated: The Yokai Encyclopedias of Toriyama Sekien | publisher = Dover Publications | year = 2017 | page = vii | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Hj9-rgEACAAJ | isbn = 978-0-4868-0035-6 }}</ref>
Sekien is best known for his mass-produced illustrated books of [[:en:Yōkai|Yōkai]] that had appeared in ''[[:en:Hyakki Yagyō|Hyakki Yagyō]]'' monster parade scrolls. The first book proved popular enough to spawn three sequels, the last of which features yōkai mainly out of Sekien's imagination. Although sometimes described as a "demonologist," his work is better described as a literary parody of encyclopedias such as the Japanese ''[[:en:Wakan Sansai Zue|Wakan Sansai Zue]]'' or the Chinese ''[[:en:Classic of Mountains and Seas|Classic of Mountains and Seas]]'', which were popular in Japan at the time.<ref>{{cite book | last1 = Yoda | first1 = Hiroko | last2 = Alt | first2 = Matt | title = Japandemonium Illustrated: The Yokai Encyclopedias of Toriyama Sekien | publisher = Dover Publications | year = 2017 | pages = viii-ix| url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Hj9-rgEACAAJ | isbn = 978-0-4868-0035-6 }}</ref> His portrayals of these creatures from folklore essentially established their visual portrayals in the public's mind and deeply inspired other Japanese artists in his own and later eras, including ukiyo-e artists [[:en:Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi]], [[:en:Kawanabe Kyōsai|Kawanabe Kyōsai]], and [[:en:Manga|Manga]] artist [[:en:Mizuki Shigeru|Mizuki Shigeru]].<ref>{{cite book | last1 = Yoda | first1 = Hiroko | last2 = Alt | first2 = Matt | title = Japandemonium Illustrated: The Yokai Encyclopedias of Toriyama Sekien | publisher = Dover Publications | year = 2017 | page = ix | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Hj9-rgEACAAJ | isbn = 978-0-4868-0035-6 }}</ref>
==Notable works== *{{nihongo|''[[Toriyamabiko]]''|鳥山彦|extra=published in 1773}} *{{nihongo|''[[Sekien Gafu|Sekien's Picture Album]]''|石燕画譜|extra=published in 1774}} *{{nihongo|''[[:en:Gazu Hyakki Yagyō|The Illustrated Demon Horde's Night Parade]]''|画図百鬼夜行|extra=published in 1776}} *{{nihongo|''[[:en:Konjaku Gazu Zoku Hyakki|The Illustrated Demon Horde from Past and Present, Continued]]''|今昔画図続百鬼|extra=published in 1779}} *{{nihongo|''[[:en:Konjaku Hyakki Shūi|More of the Demon Horde from Past and Present]]''|今昔百鬼拾遺|extra=published in 1780}} *Kawataro 河太郎 *Kawako 河腰 *Yamawaro 山和露 *Ushi no koku mairi 牛の国参り * * *{{nihongo|''[[:en:Gazu Hyakki Tsurezure Bukuro|A Horde of Haunted Housewares]]''|画図百鬼徒然袋|extra=published in 1784}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://www.mfa.org/collections/object/toriyamabiko-528443 Toriyamabiko], a copy of his first book, in the collection of the Museum of Fine Arts, Boston *[http://libmma.contentdm.oclc.org/cdm/compoundobject/collection/p15324coll10/id/153737 Bridge of dreams: the Mary Griggs Burke collection of Japanese art], a catalog from The Metropolitan Museum of Art Libraries (fully available online as PDF), which contains material on Toriyama Sekien (see index)

{{ill|Konjaku Hyakki Shūi|WD=Q2703425}}|native_name=鳥山石燕|resting_place={{ill|Kōmyō-ji|WD=Q111974576}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q3284204]] Wikidata name: Toriyama Sekien [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese artist (1712–1788)}} |italic=no}}, specifically a [[:en:Miage-nyūdō|Miage-nyūdō]], as portrayed by Toriyama]] {{nihongo|'''Toriyama Sekien'''|鳥山 石燕|extra=1712 – September 22, 1788}}, real name '''Sano Toyofusa''', was a scholar, ''[[:en:Kyōka|Kyōka]]'' poet, and ''[[ukiyo-e]]'' artist of [[Japanese folklore]]. He was among the first to apply Kanō techniques to ''[[ukiyo-e]]'' printmaking, inventing key new techniques along the way, such as ''fuki-bokashi'', which allowed for replicating color gradations.<ref>{{cite book | last = Davis | first = Julie | title = Utamaro and the Spectacle of Beauty | publisher = University of Hawaii Press | year = 2007 | page = 33 | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=oZTrAAAAMAAJ&q=fuki-bokashi+sekien | isbn = 978-0-8248-3199-8 }}</ref> Most famously, he was the teacher of [[:en:Kitagawa Utamaro|Kitagawa Utamaro]] and [[:en:Utagawa Toyoharu|Utagawa Toyoharu]].<ref>{{cite book | last1 = Yoda | first1 = Hiroko | last2 = Alt | first2 = Matt |title = Japandemonium Illustrated: The Yokai Encyclopedias of Toriyama Sekien | publisher = Dover Publications | year = 2017 | page = vii | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Hj9-rgEACAAJ | isbn = 978-0-4868-0035-6 }}</ref>
Sekien is best known for his mass-produced illustrated books of [[:en:Yōkai|Yōkai]] that had appeared in ''[[:en:Hyakki Yagyō|Hyakki Yagyō]]'' monster parade scrolls. {{ill|Konjaku Hyakki Shūi|WD=Q2703425}}|native_name=鳥山石燕|resting_place={{ill|Kōmyō-ji|WD=Q111974576}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q3284204]] Wikidata name: Toriyama Sekien [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese artist (1712–1788)}} |italic=no}}, specifically a [[:en:Miage-nyūdō|Miage-nyūdō]], as portrayed by Toriyama]] {{nihongo|'''Toriyama Sekien'''|鳥山 石燕|extra=1712 – September 22, 1788}}, real name '''Sano Toyofusa''', was a scholar, ''[[:en:Kyōka|Kyōka]]'' poet, and ''[[ukiyo-e]]'' artist of [[Japanese folklore]]. Born to a family of high-Ranking servants to the [[Tokugawa shogunate]], he was trained by [[:en:Kanō school|Kanō school]] artists Kanō Gyokuen and Kanō Chikanobu, although he was never officially recognized as a Kanō school painter.<ref>{{cite book | last = Davis | first = Julie | title = Partners in Print: Artistic Collaboration and the Ukiyo-e Market | publisher = University of Hawaii Press | year = 2015 | page = 25 | url=http://www.uhpress.hawaii.edu/p-9337-9780824839383.aspx | isbn = 978-0-8248-3938-3 }}</ref>
==Art career== After retiring from service to the shogunate, he became a teacher to numerous apprentices in poetry and painting. He was among the first to apply Kanō techniques to ''[[ukiyo-e]]'' printmaking, inventing key new techniques along the way, such as ''fuki-bokashi'', which allowed for replicating color gradations.<ref>{{cite book | last = Davis | first = Julie | title = Utamaro and the Spectacle of Beauty | publisher = University of Hawaii Press | year = 2007 | page = 33 | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=oZTrAAAAMAAJ&q=fuki-bokashi+sekien | isbn = 978-0-8248-3199-8 }}</ref> Most famously, he was the teacher of [[:en:Kitagawa Utamaro|Kitagawa Utamaro]] and [[:en:Utagawa Toyoharu|Utagawa Toyoharu]].<ref>{{cite book | last1 = Yoda | first1 = Hiroko | last2 = Alt | first2 = Matt |title = Japandemonium Illustrated: The Yokai Encyclopedias of Toriyama Sekien | publisher = Dover Publications | year = 2017 | page = vii | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Hj9-rgEACAAJ | isbn = 978-0-4868-0035-6 }}</ref>
Sekien is best known for his mass-produced illustrated books of [[:en:Yōkai|Yōkai]] that had appeared in ''[[:en:Hyakki Yagyō|Hyakki Yagyō]]'' monster parade scrolls. The first book proved popular enough to spawn three sequels, the last of which features yōkai mainly out of Sekien's imagination. Although sometimes described as a "demonologist," his work is better described as a literary parody of encyclopedias such as the Japanese ''[[:en:Wakan Sansai Zue|Wakan Sansai Zue]]'' or the Chinese ''[[:en:Classic of Mountains and Seas|Classic of Mountains and Seas]]'', which were popular in Japan at the time.<ref>{{cite book | last1 = Yoda | first1 = Hiroko | last2 = Alt | first2 = Matt | title = Japandemonium Illustrated: The Yokai Encyclopedias of Toriyama Sekien | publisher = Dover Publications | year = 2017 | pages = viii-ix| url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Hj9-rgEACAAJ | isbn = 978-0-4868-0035-6 }}</ref> His portrayals of these creatures from folklore essentially established their visual portrayals in the public's mind and deeply inspired other Japanese artists in his own and later eras, including ukiyo-e artists [[:en:Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi]], [[:en:Kawanabe Kyōsai|Kawanabe Kyōsai]], and [[:en:Manga|Manga]] artist [[:en:Mizuki Shigeru|Mizuki Shigeru]].<ref>{{cite book | last1 = Yoda | first1 = Hiroko | last2 = Alt | first2 = Matt | title = Japandemonium Illustrated: The Yokai Encyclopedias of Toriyama Sekien | publisher = Dover Publications | year = 2017 | page = ix | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Hj9-rgEACAAJ | isbn = 978-0-4868-0035-6 }}</ref>
==Notable works== *{{nihongo|''[[Toriyamabiko]]''|鳥山彦|extra=published in 1773}} *{{nihongo|''[[Sekien Gafu|Sekien's Picture Album]]''|石燕画譜|extra=published in 1774}} *{{nihongo|''[[:en:Gazu Hyakki Yagyō|The Illustrated Demon Horde's Night Parade]]''|画図百鬼夜行|extra=published in 1776}} *{{nihongo|''[[:en:Konjaku Gazu Zoku Hyakki|The Illustrated Demon Horde from Past and Present, Continued]]''|今昔画図続百鬼|extra=published in 1779}} *{{nihongo|''[[:en:Konjaku Hyakki Shūi|More of the Demon Horde from Past and Present]]''|今昔百鬼拾遺|extra=published in 1780}} *Kawataro 河太郎 *Kawako 河腰 *Yamawaro 山和露 *Ushi no koku mairi 牛の国参り * * *{{nihongo|''[[:en:Gazu Hyakki Tsurezure Bukuro|A Horde of Haunted Housewares]]''|画図百鬼徒然袋|extra=published in 1784}}

The first book proved popular enough to spawn three sequels, the last of which features yōkai mainly out of Sekien's imagination. ==Notable works== *{{nihongo|''[[Toriyamabiko]]''|鳥山彦|extra=published in 1773}} *{{nihongo|''[[Sekien Gafu|Sekien's Picture Album]]''|石燕画譜|extra=published in 1774}} *{{nihongo|''[[:en:Gazu Hyakki Yagyō|The Illustrated Demon Horde's Night Parade]]''|画図百鬼夜行|extra=published in 1776}} *{{nihongo|''[[:en:Konjaku Gazu Zoku Hyakki|The Illustrated Demon Horde from Past and Present, Continued]]''|今昔画図続百鬼|extra=published in 1779}} *{{nihongo|''[[:en:Konjaku Hyakki Shūi|More of the Demon Horde from Past and Present]]''|今昔百鬼拾遺|extra=published in 1780}} *Kawataro 河太郎 *Kawako 河腰 *Yamawaro 山和露 *Ushi no koku mairi 牛の国参り * * *{{nihongo|''[[:en:Gazu Hyakki Tsurezure Bukuro|A Horde of Haunted Housewares]]''|画図百鬼徒然袋|extra=published in 1784}}

{{ill|painter|WD=Q1028181}}; {{ill|ukiyo-e artist|WD=Q33082999}}|notable_works={{ill|Gazu Hyakki Tsurezure Bukuro|WD=Q2703856}}; {{ill|Gazu Hyakki Yagyō|WD=Q2696876}}; {{ill|Konjaku Gazu Zoku Hyakki|WD=Q2703416}}; {{ill|Konjaku Hyakki Shūi|WD=Q2703425}}|native_name=鳥山石燕|resting_place={{ill|Kōmyō-ji|WD=Q111974576}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q3284204]] Wikidata name: Toriyama Sekien [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese artist (1712–1788)}} |italic=no}}, specifically a [[:en:Miage-nyūdō|Miage-nyūdō]], as portrayed by Toriyama]] {{nihongo|'''Toriyama Sekien'''|鳥山 石燕|extra=1712 – September 22, 1788}}, real name '''Sano Toyofusa''', was a scholar, ''[[:en:Kyōka|Kyōka]]'' poet, and ''[[ukiyo-e]]'' artist of [[Japanese folklore]]. Born to a family of high-Ranking servants to the [[Tokugawa shogunate]], he was trained by [[:en:Kanō school|Kanō school]] artists Kanō Gyokuen and Kanō Chikanobu, although he was never officially recognized as a Kanō school painter.<ref>{{cite book | last = Davis | first = Julie | title = Partners in Print: Artistic Collaboration and the Ukiyo-e Market | publisher = University of Hawaii Press | year = 2015 | page = 25 | url=http://www.uhpress.hawaii.edu/p-9337-9780824839383.aspx | isbn = 978-0-8248-3938-3 }}</ref>
==Art career== After retiring from service to the shogunate, he became a teacher to numerous apprentices in poetry and painting. He was among the first to apply Kanō techniques to ''[[ukiyo-e]]'' printmaking, inventing key new techniques along the way, such as ''fuki-bokashi'', which allowed for replicating color gradations.<ref>{{cite book | last = Davis | first = Julie | title = Utamaro and the Spectacle of Beauty | publisher = University of Hawaii Press | year = 2007 | page = 33 | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=oZTrAAAAMAAJ&q=fuki-bokashi+sekien | isbn = 978-0-8248-3199-8 }}</ref> Most famously, he was the teacher of [[:en:Kitagawa Utamaro|Kitagawa Utamaro]] and [[:en:Utagawa Toyoharu|Utagawa Toyoharu]].<ref>{{cite book | last1 = Yoda | first1 = Hiroko | last2 = Alt | first2 = Matt |title = Japandemonium Illustrated: The Yokai Encyclopedias of Toriyama Sekien | publisher = Dover Publications | year = 2017 | page = vii | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Hj9-rgEACAAJ | isbn = 978-0-4868-0035-6 }}</ref>
Sekien is best known for his mass-produced illustrated books of [[:en:Yōkai|Yōkai]] that had appeared in ''[[:en:Hyakki Yagyō|Hyakki Yagyō]]'' monster parade scrolls. The first book proved popular enough to spawn three sequels, the last of which features yōkai mainly out of Sekien's imagination. Although sometimes described as a "demonologist," his work is better described as a literary parody of encyclopedias such as the Japanese ''[[:en:Wakan Sansai Zue|Wakan Sansai Zue]]'' or the Chinese ''[[:en:Classic of Mountains and Seas|Classic of Mountains and Seas]]'', which were popular in Japan at the time.<ref>{{cite book | last1 = Yoda | first1 = Hiroko | last2 = Alt | first2 = Matt | title = Japandemonium Illustrated: The Yokai Encyclopedias of Toriyama Sekien | publisher = Dover Publications | year = 2017 | pages = viii-ix| url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Hj9-rgEACAAJ | isbn = 978-0-4868-0035-6 }}</ref> His portrayals of these creatures from folklore essentially established their visual portrayals in the public's mind and deeply inspired other Japanese artists in his own and later eras, including ukiyo-e artists [[:en:Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi]], [[:en:Kawanabe Kyōsai|Kawanabe Kyōsai]], and [[:en:Manga|Manga]] artist [[:en:Mizuki Shigeru|Mizuki Shigeru]].<ref>{{cite book | last1 = Yoda | first1 = Hiroko | last2 = Alt | first2 = Matt | title = Japandemonium Illustrated: The Yokai Encyclopedias of Toriyama Sekien | publisher = Dover Publications | year = 2017 | page = ix | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Hj9-rgEACAAJ | isbn = 978-0-4868-0035-6 }}</ref>
==Notable works== *{{nihongo|''[[Toriyamabiko]]''|鳥山彦|extra=published in 1773}} *{{nihongo|''[[Sekien Gafu|Sekien's Picture Album]]''|石燕画譜|extra=published in 1774}} *{{nihongo|''[[:en:Gazu Hyakki Yagyō|The Illustrated Demon Horde's Night Parade]]''|画図百鬼夜行|extra=published in 1776}} *{{nihongo|''[[:en:Konjaku Gazu Zoku Hyakki|The Illustrated Demon Horde from Past and Present, Continued]]''|今昔画図続百鬼|extra=published in 1779}} *{{nihongo|''[[:en:Konjaku Hyakki Shūi|More of the Demon Horde from Past and Present]]''|今昔百鬼拾遺|extra=published in 1780}} *Kawataro 河太郎 *Kawako 河腰 *Yamawaro 山和露 *Ushi no koku mairi 牛の国参り * * *{{nihongo|''[[:en:Gazu Hyakki Tsurezure Bukuro|A Horde of Haunted Housewares]]''|画図百鬼徒然袋|extra=published in 1784}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[bcl:Toriyama Sekien]][[bg:Торияма Секиен]][[ca:Toriyama Sekien]][[de:Toriyama Sekien]][[el:Τοριγιάμα Σέκιεν]][[en:Toriyama Sekien]][[es:Toriyama Sekien]][[fa:توری‌یاما سکیئن]][[fr:Toriyama Sekien]][[id:Toriyama Sekien]][[it:Toriyama Sekien]][[ja:鳥山石燕]][[ko:도리야마 세키엔]][[nl:Toriyama Sekien]][[no:Toriyama Sekien]][[pl:Sekien Toriyama]][[pt:Toriyama Sekien]][[ru:Торияма Сэкиэн]][[tr:Toriyama Seiken]][[uk:Торіяма Секіен]][[vi:Toriyama Sekien]][[zh:鳥山石燕]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[bcl:Toriyama Sekien]][[bg:Торияма Секиен]][[ca:Toriyama Sekien]][[de:Toriyama Sekien]][[el:Τοριγιάμα Σέκιεν]][[en:Toriyama Sekien]][[es:Toriyama Sekien]][[fa:توری‌یاما سکیئن]][[fr:Toriyama Sekien]][[id:Toriyama Sekien]][[it:Toriyama Sekien]][[ja:鳥山石燕]][[ko:도리야마 세키엔]][[nl:Toriyama Sekien]][[no:Toriyama Sekien]][[pl:Sekien Toriyama]][[pt:Toriyama Sekien]][[ru:Торияма Сэкиэн]][[tr:Toriyama Seiken]][[uk:Торіяма Секіен]][[vi:Toriyama Sekien]][[zh:鳥山石燕]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[bcl:Toriyama Sekien]][[bg:Торияма Секиен]][[ca:Toriyama Sekien]][[de:Toriyama Sekien]][[el:Τοριγιάμα Σέκιεν]][[en:Toriyama Sekien]][[es:Toriyama Sekien]][[fa:توری‌یاما سکیئن]][[fr:Toriyama Sekien]][[id:Toriyama Sekien]][[it:Toriyama Sekien]][[ja:鳥山石燕]][[ko:도리야마 세키엔]][[nl:Toriyama Sekien]][[no:Toriyama Sekien]][[pl:Sekien Toriyama]][[pt:Toriyama Sekien]][[ru:Торияма Сэкиэн]][[tr:Toriyama Seiken]][[uk:Торіяма Секіен]][[vi:Toriyama Sekien]][[zh:鳥山石燕]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1712|death_date={{death date|1788|9|22}}|death_place={{ill|Kamakura|WD=Q200267}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|haiku poet|WD=Q11386386}}; {{ill|painter|WD=Q1028181}}; {{ill|ukiyo-e artist|WD=Q33082999}}|notable_works={{ill|Gazu Hyakki Tsurezure Bukuro|WD=Q2703856}}; {{ill|Gazu Hyakki Yagyō|WD=Q2696876}}; {{ill|Konjaku Gazu Zoku Hyakki|WD=Q2703416}}; {{ill|Konjaku Hyakki Shūi|WD=Q2703425}}|native_name=鳥山石燕|resting_place={{ill|Kōmyō-ji|WD=Q111974576}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q3284204]] Wikidata name: Toriyama Sekien [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese artist (1712–1788)}} |italic=no}}, specifically a {{ill|Miage-nyūdō|en|Miage-nyūdō|WD=Q6149361|lt=Miage-nyūdō}}, as portrayed by Toriyama]] {{nihongo|'''Toriyama Sekien'''|鳥山 石燕|extra=1712 – September 22, 1788}}, real name '''Sano Toyofusa''', was a scholar, ''{{ill|Kyōka|en|Kyōka|WD=Q3201017|lt=Kyōka}}'' poet, and ''[[ukiyo-e]]'' artist of [[Japanese folklore]]. Born to a family of high-Ranking servants to the [[Tokugawa shogunate]], he was trained by {{ill|Kanō school|en|Kanō school|WD=Q252801|lt=Kanō school}} artists Kanō Gyokuen and Kanō Chikanobu, although he was never officially recognized as a Kanō school painter.<ref>{{cite book | last = Davis | first = Julie | title = Partners in Print: Artistic Collaboration and the Ukiyo-e Market | publisher = University of Hawaii Press | year = 2015 | page = 25 | url=http://www.uhpress.hawaii.edu/p-9337-9780824839383.aspx | isbn = 978-0-8248-3938-3 }}</ref>
==Art career== After retiring from service to the shogunate, he became a teacher to numerous apprentices in poetry and painting. He was among the first to apply Kanō techniques to ''[[ukiyo-e]]'' printmaking, inventing key new techniques along the way, such as ''fuki-bokashi'', which allowed for replicating color gradations.<ref>{{cite book | last = Davis | first = Julie | title = Utamaro and the Spectacle of Beauty | publisher = University of Hawaii Press | year = 2007 | page = 33 | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=oZTrAAAAMAAJ&q=fuki-bokashi+sekien | isbn = 978-0-8248-3199-8 }}</ref> Most famously, he was the teacher of [[:en:Kitagawa Utamaro|Kitagawa Utamaro]] and {{ill|Utagawa Toyoharu|en|Utagawa Toyoharu|WD=Q3109421|lt=Utagawa Toyoharu}}.<ref>{{cite book | last1 = Yoda | first1 = Hiroko | last2 = Alt | first2 = Matt |title = Japandemonium Illustrated: The Yokai Encyclopedias of Toriyama Sekien | publisher = Dover Publications | year = 2017 | page = vii | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Hj9-rgEACAAJ | isbn = 978-0-4868-0035-6 }}</ref>
Sekien is best known for his mass-produced illustrated books of {{ill|Yōkai|en|Yōkai|WD=Q503186|lt=Yōkai}} that had appeared in ''{{ill|Hyakki Yagyō|en|Hyakki Yagyō|WD=Q1358278|lt=Hyakki Yagyō}}'' monster parade scrolls. The first book proved popular enough to spawn three sequels, the last of which features yōkai mainly out of Sekien's imagination. Although sometimes described as a "demonologist," his work is better described as a literary parody of encyclopedias such as the Japanese ''{{ill|Wakan Sansai Zue|en|Wakan Sansai Zue|WD=Q1135803|lt=Wakan Sansai Zue}}'' or the Chinese ''{{ill|Classic of Mountains and Seas|en|Classic of Mountains and Seas|WD=Q844278|lt=Classic of Mountains and Seas}}'', which were popular in Japan at the time.<ref>{{cite book | last1 = Yoda | first1 = Hiroko | last2 = Alt | first2 = Matt | title = Japandemonium Illustrated: The Yokai Encyclopedias of Toriyama Sekien | publisher = Dover Publications | year = 2017 | pages = viii-ix| url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Hj9-rgEACAAJ | isbn = 978-0-4868-0035-6 }}</ref> His portrayals of these creatures from folklore essentially established their visual portrayals in the public's mind and deeply inspired other Japanese artists in his own and later eras, including ukiyo-e artists {{ill|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|en|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|WD=Q467337|lt=Tsukioka Yoshitoshi}}, {{ill|Kawanabe Kyōsai|en|Kawanabe Kyōsai|WD=Q2838030|lt=Kawanabe Kyōsai}}, and {{ill|Manga|en|Manga|WD=Q8274|lt=Manga}} artist [[:en:Mizuki Shigeru|Mizuki Shigeru]].<ref>{{cite book | last1 = Yoda | first1 = Hiroko | last2 = Alt | first2 = Matt | title = Japandemonium Illustrated: The Yokai Encyclopedias of Toriyama Sekien | publisher = Dover Publications | year = 2017 | page = ix | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Hj9-rgEACAAJ | isbn = 978-0-4868-0035-6 }}</ref>
==Notable works== *{{nihongo|''[[Toriyamabiko]]''|鳥山彦|extra=published in 1773}} *{{nihongo|''[[Sekien Gafu|Sekien's Picture Album]]''|石燕画譜|extra=published in 1774}} *{{nihongo|''{{ill|Gazu Hyakki Yagyō|en|Gazu Hyakki Yagyō|WD=Q2696876|lt=The Illustrated Demon Horde's Night Parade}}''|画図百鬼夜行|extra=published in 1776}} *{{nihongo|''{{ill|Konjaku Gazu Zoku Hyakki|en|Konjaku Gazu Zoku Hyakki|WD=Q2703416|lt=The Illustrated Demon Horde from Past and Present, Continued}}''|今昔画図続百鬼|extra=published in 1779}} *{{nihongo|''{{ill|Konjaku Hyakki Shūi|en|Konjaku Hyakki Shūi|WD=Q2703425|lt=More of the Demon Horde from Past and Present}}''|今昔百鬼拾遺|extra=published in 1780}} *Kawataro 河太郎 *Kawako 河腰 *Yamawaro 山和露 *Ushi no koku mairi 牛の国参り * * *{{nihongo|''{{ill|Gazu Hyakki Tsurezure Bukuro|en|Gazu Hyakki Tsurezure Bukuro|WD=Q2703856|lt=A Horde of Haunted Housewares}}''|画図百鬼徒然袋|extra=published in 1784}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://www.mfa.org/collections/object/toriyamabiko-528443 Toriyamabiko], a copy of his first book, in the collection of the Museum of Fine Arts, Boston *[http://libmma.contentdm.oclc.org/cdm/compoundobject/collection/p15324coll10/id/153737 Bridge of dreams: the Mary Griggs Burke collection of Japanese art], a catalog from The Metropolitan Museum of Art Libraries (fully available online as PDF), which contains material on Toriyama Sekien (see index)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[bcl:Toriyama Sekien]][[bg:Торияма Секиен]][[ca:Toriyama Sekien]][[de:Toriyama Sekien]][[el:Τοριγιάμα Σέκιεν]][[en:Toriyama Sekien]][[es:Toriyama Sekien]][[fa:توری‌یاما سکیئن]][[fr:Toriyama Sekien]][[id:Toriyama Sekien]][[it:Toriyama Sekien]][[ja:鳥山石燕]][[ko:도리야마 세키엔]][[nl:Toriyama Sekien]][[no:Toriyama Sekien]][[pl:Sekien Toriyama]][[pt:Toriyama Sekien]][[ru:Торияма Сэкиэн]][[tr:Toriyama Seiken]][[uk:Торіяма Секіен]][[vi:Toriyama Sekien]][[zh:鳥山石燕]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[bcl:Toriyama Sekien]][[bg:Торияма Секиен]][[ca:Toriyama Sekien]][[de:Toriyama Sekien]][[el:Τοριγιάμα Σέκιεν]][[en:Toriyama Sekien]][[es:Toriyama Sekien]][[fa:توری‌یاما سکیئن]][[fr:Toriyama Sekien]][[id:Toriyama Sekien]][[it:Toriyama Sekien]][[ja:鳥山石燕]][[ko:도리야마 세키엔]][[nl:Toriyama Sekien]][[no:Toriyama Sekien]][[pl:Sekien Toriyama]][[pt:Toriyama Sekien]][[ru:Торияма Сэкиэн]][[tr:Toriyama Seiken]][[uk:Торіяма Секіен]][[vi:Toriyama Sekien]][[zh:鳥山石燕]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[bcl:Toriyama Sekien]][[bg:Торияма Секиен]][[ca:Toriyama Sekien]][[de:Toriyama Sekien]][[el:Τοριγιάμα Σέκιεν]][[en:Toriyama Sekien]][[es:Toriyama Sekien]][[fa:توری‌یاما سکیئن]][[fr:Toriyama Sekien]][[id:Toriyama Sekien]][[it:Toriyama Sekien]][[ja:鳥山石燕]][[ko:도리야마 세키엔]][[nl:Toriyama Sekien]][[no:Toriyama Sekien]][[pl:Sekien Toriyama]][[pt:Toriyama Sekien]][[ru:Торияма Сэкиэн]][[tr:Toriyama Seiken]][[uk:Торіяма Секіен]][[vi:Toriyama Sekien]][[zh:鳥山石燕]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1712|death_date={{death date|1788|9|22}}|death_place={{ill|Kamakura|WD=Q200267}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|haiku poet|WD=Q11386386}}; {{ill|painter|WD=Q1028181}}; {{ill|ukiyo-e artist|WD=Q33082999}}|notable_works={{ill|Gazu Hyakki Tsurezure Bukuro|WD=Q2703856}}; {{ill|Gazu Hyakki Yagyō|WD=Q2696876}}; {{ill|Konjaku Gazu Zoku Hyakki|WD=Q2703416}}; {{ill|Konjaku Hyakki Shūi|WD=Q2703425}}|native_name=鳥山石燕|resting_place={{ill|Kōmyō-ji|WD=Q111974576}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q3284204]] Wikidata name: Toriyama Sekien [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese artist (1712–1788)}} |italic=no}}, specifically a {{ill|Miage-nyūdō|en|Miage-nyūdō|WD=Q6149361|lt=Miage-nyūdō}}, as portrayed by Toriyama]] {{nihongo|'''Toriyama Sekien'''|鳥山 石燕|extra=1712 – September 22, 1788}}, real name '''Sano Toyofusa''', was a scholar, ''{{ill|Kyōka|en|Kyōka|WD=Q3201017|lt=Kyōka}}'' poet, and ''[[ukiyo-e]]'' artist of [[Japanese folklore]]. Born to a family of high-Ranking servants to the [[Tokugawa shogunate]], he was trained by {{ill|Kanō school|en|Kanō school|WD=Q252801|lt=Kanō school}} artists Kanō Gyokuen and Kanō Chikanobu, although he was never officially recognized as a Kanō school painter.<ref>{{cite book | last = Davis | first = Julie | title = Partners in Print: Artistic Collaboration and the Ukiyo-e Market | publisher = University of Hawaii Press | year = 2015 | page = 25 | url=http://www.uhpress.hawaii.edu/p-9337-9780824839383.aspx | isbn = 978-0-8248-3938-3 }}</ref>
==Art career== After retiring from service to the shogunate, he became a teacher to numerous apprentices in poetry and painting. He was among the first to apply Kanō techniques to ''[[ukiyo-e]]'' printmaking, inventing key new techniques along the way, such as ''fuki-bokashi'', which allowed for replicating color gradations.<ref>{{cite book | last = Davis | first = Julie | title = Utamaro and the Spectacle of Beauty | publisher = University of Hawaii Press | year = 2007 | page = 33 | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=oZTrAAAAMAAJ&q=fuki-bokashi+sekien | isbn = 978-0-8248-3199-8 }}</ref> Most famously, he was the teacher of [[:en:Kitagawa Utamaro|Kitagawa Utamaro]] and {{ill|Utagawa Toyoharu|en|Utagawa Toyoharu|WD=Q3109421|lt=Utagawa Toyoharu}}.<ref>{{cite book | last1 = Yoda | first1 = Hiroko | last2 = Alt | first2 = Matt |title = Japandemonium Illustrated: The Yokai Encyclopedias of Toriyama Sekien | publisher = Dover Publications | year = 2017 | page = vii | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Hj9-rgEACAAJ | isbn = 978-0-4868-0035-6 }}</ref>
Sekien is best known for his mass-produced illustrated books of {{ill|Yōkai|en|Yōkai|WD=Q503186|lt=Yōkai}} that had appeared in ''{{ill|Hyakki Yagyō|en|Hyakki Yagyō|WD=Q1358278|lt=Hyakki Yagyō}}'' monster parade scrolls. The first book proved popular enough to spawn three sequels, the last of which features yōkai mainly out of Sekien's imagination. Although sometimes described as a "demonologist," his work is better described as a literary parody of encyclopedias such as the Japanese ''{{ill|Wakan Sansai Zue|en|Wakan Sansai Zue|WD=Q1135803|lt=Wakan Sansai Zue}}'' or the Chinese ''{{ill|Classic of Mountains and Seas|en|Classic of Mountains and Seas|WD=Q844278|lt=Classic of Mountains and Seas}}'', which were popular in Japan at the time.<ref>{{cite book | last1 = Yoda | first1 = Hiroko | last2 = Alt | first2 = Matt | title = Japandemonium Illustrated: The Yokai Encyclopedias of Toriyama Sekien | publisher = Dover Publications | year = 2017 | pages = viii-ix| url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Hj9-rgEACAAJ | isbn = 978-0-4868-0035-6 }}</ref> His portrayals of these creatures from folklore essentially established their visual portrayals in the public's mind and deeply inspired other Japanese artists in his own and later eras, including ukiyo-e artists {{ill|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|en|Tsukioka Yoshitoshi|WD=Q467337|lt=Tsukioka Yoshitoshi}}, {{ill|Kawanabe Kyōsai|en|Kawanabe Kyōsai|WD=Q2838030|lt=Kawanabe Kyōsai}}, and {{ill|Manga|en|Manga|WD=Q8274|lt=Manga}} artist [[:en:Mizuki Shigeru|Mizuki Shigeru]].<ref>{{cite book | last1 = Yoda | first1 = Hiroko | last2 = Alt | first2 = Matt | title = Japandemonium Illustrated: The Yokai Encyclopedias of Toriyama Sekien | publisher = Dover Publications | year = 2017 | page = ix | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Hj9-rgEACAAJ | isbn = 978-0-4868-0035-6 }}</ref>
==Notable works== *{{nihongo|''[[Toriyamabiko]]''|鳥山彦|extra=published in 1773}} *{{nihongo|''[[Sekien Gafu|Sekien's Picture Album]]''|石燕画譜|extra=published in 1774}} *{{nihongo|''{{ill|Gazu Hyakki Yagyō|en|Gazu Hyakki Yagyō|WD=Q2696876|lt=The Illustrated Demon Horde's Night Parade}}''|画図百鬼夜行|extra=published in 1776}} *{{nihongo|''{{ill|Konjaku Gazu Zoku Hyakki|en|Konjaku Gazu Zoku Hyakki|WD=Q2703416|lt=The Illustrated Demon Horde from Past and Present, Continued}}''|今昔画図続百鬼|extra=published in 1779}} *{{nihongo|''{{ill|Konjaku Hyakki Shūi|en|Konjaku Hyakki Shūi|WD=Q2703425|lt=More of the Demon Horde from Past and Present}}''|今昔百鬼拾遺|extra=published in 1780}} *Kawataro 河太郎 *Kawako 河腰 *Yamawaro 山和露 *Ushi no koku mairi 牛の国参り * * *{{nihongo|''{{ill|Gazu Hyakki Tsurezure Bukuro|en|Gazu Hyakki Tsurezure Bukuro|WD=Q2703856|lt=A Horde of Haunted Housewares}}''|画図百鬼徒然袋|extra=published in 1784}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://www.mfa.org/collections/object/toriyamabiko-528443 Toriyamabiko], a copy of his first book, in the collection of the Museum of Fine Arts, Boston *[http://libmma.contentdm.oclc.org/cdm/compoundobject/collection/p15324coll10/id/153737 Bridge of dreams: the Mary Griggs Burke collection of Japanese art], a catalog from The Metropolitan Museum of Art Libraries (fully available online as PDF), which contains material on Toriyama Sekien (see index)
